---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size60[.center[

Welcome to our local stakeholders working on environmental policy!

]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Save ten minutes at end for assignment for next week



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size65[**Today**]

## Kane Sheek
.size40[
+ Sierra Club - White River Group
+ Ready for 100 Springfield Community Organizer
]

## Todd Wilkinson
.size40[
+ James River Basin Partnership
+ Project Manager
]

???

We have a few goals for our discussion on Thursday

1. Familiarize ourselves with local environmental groups (stakeholders), and

2. Gather some info to help us with our paper projects.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size60[**For Thursday: Writing Workshop**]

.size40[
1. Choose your environmental problem,

2. Outline your paper, and

3. Find evidence to support each premise in your proposed argument
]

???

Let's get to work on the paper!

Everybody brings in a case (or two for EC!) we can discuss on Tuesday.

<br>

### Questions?
